title: [BUG] dataValidation formula returning NaN
author: nforbush

comments.author: nforbush
comments.body:
This is more complicated...

1) Of course, since parseInt or parseFloat can return 0 and then would return formula, the correct syntax (in data-validations-xform) would be something like:
                    let formula = this._formula.join('');
                    let formula1 = Number.NaN;  // *** ADDED ***
                    switch (this._dataValidation.type) {
                    case 'whole':
                    case 'textLength':
                        formula1 = parseInt(formula, 10); // *** MODIFIED ***
                        break;
                    case 'decimal':
                        formula1 = parseFloat(formula); // *** MODIFIED ***
                        break;
                    case 'date':
                        formula1 = utils.excelToDate(parseFloat(formula)); // *** MODIFIED ***
                        break;
                    default:
                        break;
                }
                formula = isNaN(formula1) ? formula : formula1; // *** ADDED ***
                this._dataValidation.formulae.push(formula);

2) I have this example where I33:I34 is noted twice...the first one is correct... but that means should only add to the model if the address is undefined, e.g. if (this.model[address] === undefined) this.model[address] = this._dataValidation;

    <dataValidations count="5">
        <dataValidation type="whole" operator="greaterThanOrEqual" allowBlank="1" showInputMessage="1"
                        showErrorMessage="1" sqref="P24:P25 I28 **I33:I34"**
                        xr:uid="{442302FA-720E-4FC7-9F29-E0D3BD82100B}">
            <formula1>0</formula1>
        </dataValidation>
        <dataValidation type="list" allowBlank="1" showInputMessage="1" showErrorMessage="1" sqref="H21:I21"
                        xr:uid="{00000000-0002-0000-0A00-000001000000}">
            <formula1>varCurrencies</formula1>
        </dataValidation>
        <dataValidation type="whole" operator="greaterThanOrEqual" allowBlank="1" showInputMessage="1"
                        showErrorMessage="1" sqref="P26:P27 P29" xr:uid="{3B447C98-5CB4-4B2A-A1ED-9D6558C6CE3F}">
            <formula1>L26</formula1>
        </dataValidation>
        <dataValidation operator="greaterThanOrEqual" allowBlank="1" showInputMessage="1" showErrorMessage="1"
                        sqref="I33:I34" xr:uid="{C1D1FD86-CFEC-4D22-B3A7-EC393A513CC9}"/>
        <dataValidation type="whole" operator="greaterThanOrEqual" allowBlank="1" showInputMessage="1"
                        showErrorMessage="1" sqref=**"I33:I34"** xr:uid="{65461910-E85E-4D72-B896-6246E34DFB25}">
            <formula1>10</formula1>
        </dataValidation>
    </dataValidations>

3) It gets more complicated though as Excel seemingly stores the formula in a relative way, e.g. 
        <dataValidation type="whole" operator="greaterThanOrEqual" allowBlank="1" showInputMessage="1"
                        showErrorMessage="1" sqref="P26:P27 P29" xr:uid="{3B447C98-5CB4-4B2A-A1ED-9D6558C6CE3F}">
            <formula1>L26</formula1>
        </dataValidation>

And so would need to adjust the fomula1 (L26) by the offset of first address (P26)...so that for P27, formula1 is L27...

comments.author: wadiasalem
comments.body: Any update? i have the same problem
